hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 5, Q. Your opponent raised before the cards were dealt. You called.
Turn — Jack. Your opponent bet again. You called.
River — 8. Your opponent checked — then called your bet.

wrongReasons: 2 6 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that 2 6 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
A 9 makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
With only high card, 10 K wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
hand.explanation:
Answer: Q Q

Your opponent had an overpair and bet the flop and turn for value but checked back the river — a sign they were confident earlier but wanted pot control once the board got scarier.
